en.extract:
The Andaman treepie (Dendrocitta bayleii) is a species of bird in the family Corvidae. First described by Robert Christopher Tytler in 1863, it is endemic to the Andaman Islands of India, where its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It is threatened by habitat loss.
The scientific name commemorates the Anglo-Indian statesman Edward Clive Bayley.

The '''Andaman treepie''' ('''''Dendrocitta bayleii''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Corvidae]]. First described by [[Robert Christopher Tytler]] in 1863, it is [[endemic]] to the [[Andaman Islands]] of [[India]], where its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name = "IUCN"/>

The scientific name commemorates the Anglo-Indian statesman [[Edward Clive Bayley]].

zh.extract:
安达曼树鹊（学名：Dendrocitta bayleyi），是鸦科树鹊属的一种，为印度安達曼群島的特有种。该物种的保护状况被评为近危。
安达曼树鹊的平均体重约为102.5克。栖息地为亚热带或热带的湿润低地林。
